clean_output:
article_id: art-2026-03-21-015
summary: Strong benchmark performance is visible, but the source itself shows unresolved tension between leaderboard gains and broader production usefulness.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: The reported 65.4% benchmark result is presented as strong, but the source also says rollout outcomes are unstable enough that sampled runs can differ materially, which weakens confidence in fine-grained leaderboard precision.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Augment reports that its SWE-bench Verified submission achieved a 65.4% success rate using Claude Sonnet 3.7 as the core driver and OpenAI o1 as the ensembler.
      - The source indicates that SWE-bench results are materially bounded by benchmark instability and task-domain specificity, limiting clean transfer to broader real-world coding work.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-001
      - fact-007
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-01
      - evidence-07
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: Keep visible that the headline score is self-reported and sits on top of benchmark instability, so small performance margins should not be treated as clean evidence of stable superiority.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: The source presents simple grep-and-find navigation as sufficient for SWE-bench, while also stating that the same approach is limited in real-world settings with ambiguous inputs and larger codebases.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The source indicates that SWE-bench results are materially bounded by benchmark instability and task-domain specificity, limiting clean transfer to broader real-world coding work.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-008
      - fact-009
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-08
      - evidence-09
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: Keep visible that the method fit shown on the benchmark does not cleanly transfer to production environments.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: Some changes reportedly improved production agents in qualitative customer feedback but did not improve SWE-bench scores, creating a direct tension between benchmark optimization and user-relevant performance.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The source indicates that SWE-bench results are materially bounded by benchmark instability and task-domain specificity, limiting clean transfer to broader real-world coding work.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-013
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-13
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: Keep visible that benchmark gains and practical customer value are not aligned by default in this source.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: Claude Sonnet 3.7 thinking mode did not improve SWE-bench performance, which conflicts with the common expectation that more explicit deliberation should help coding-agent outcomes.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Augment reports that Claude Sonnet 3.7 thinking mode did not improve SWE-bench performance.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-004
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-04
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: Keep visible that this is a benchmark-specific negative result; the evidence supports the failure on SWE-bench but does not explain the mechanism fully.
    confidence: medium
  - contradiction_text: The separate fix-regressions agent corrected some regressions but also introduced bugs into otherwise correct candidates, so an apparently helpful corrective layer produced offsetting harm.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The source indicates that SWE-bench results are materially bounded by benchmark instability and task-domain specificity, limiting clean transfer to broader real-world coding work.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-005
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-05
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: Keep visible that sub-agent interventions can improve one slice of performance while degrading another, leaving no net gain.
